mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then read as policy accommodation rather than recession alarm.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are negative or not above baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS has fallen for 2 consecutive months and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above a restrictive threshold, equity valuations reprice lower because the market anticipates tighter financial conditions and slower future earnings growth. This effect should be visible within roughly one trading month after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of windows beginning after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, or if the average forward return is not below zero in those windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income momentum, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors mark down cyclical earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration trigger does not correspond to negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support equity prices over the next 60 trading days through improved sales growth expectations and operating leverage.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO together with rising HOUST marks a supply-side expansion that eases inflation pressure and supports risk assets after a short lag. The signal should favor positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when output and construction activity accelerate together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in INDPRO and HOUST do not lead to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the average return is negative across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate demand that improves forward earnings expectations and supports broad equity performance over the next quarter. The effect is expected to persist beyond one month because GDP revisions and earnings forecasts update slowly.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive on a quarterly annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the relationship is not positive over a rolling 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE with continued PAYEMS expansion supports consumer income and reduces recession probability, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through faster multiple expansion in risk-on sessions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4% and PAYEMS increases month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the labor-resilience condition is not associated with positive 5-day returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% in any expanding-window test.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness fades and equities should rally over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate a pause in tightening. The effect fails if inflation re-accelerates after the deceleration.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation slows for 2 consecutive monthly prints while FEDFUNDS stays above 1%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with elevated FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if subsequent inflation re-acceleration does not remove the effect.
